resume:
Manufacturing Process Engineer — Lean | Six Sigma | TPM | SMED | OEE | Kaizen | 5S | Kanban | DMAIC | SPC | FMEA | Poka-Yoke | Andon | Value Stream Mapping | Root Cause Analysis | 8D | Gemba | Heijunka | Jidoka | Takt Time | Continuous Improvement

PROFILE
Results-driven, cross-functional, hands-on process engineer with proven expertise in lean manufacturing, operational excellence, and end-to-end continuous improvement. Passionate, detail-oriented, data-driven team player who thrives in fast-paced, high-volume environments.

CORE COMPETENCIES
Lean Manufacturing · Six Sigma Green Belt · Total Productive Maintenance · Single-Minute Exchange of Die · Overall Equipment Effectiveness · Statistical Process Control · Failure Mode and Effects Analysis · Standard Work · Line Balancing · Throughput Optimization · Scrap Reduction · Cycle-Time Reduction · Cost Avoidance

EXPERIENCE
Meridian Components (Tier-1 automotive supplier) — Process Engineer, 2020–present
Injection-molding and assembly cell, three shifts, 14 machines.
- Drove lean, TPM, SMED, OEE, kaizen, and 5S initiatives across the value stream
- Supported continuous improvement, scrap reduction, and cycle-time optimization efforts
- Partnered cross-functionally with quality, maintenance, and production teams

Halvorsen Industrial — Manufacturing Engineer (co-op then FTE), 2017–2020
- Contributed to root-cause analysis, FMEA, and SPC deployment on the stamping line

EDUCATION
B.S. Mechanical Engineering — State Polytechnic University, 2017
ASQ Six Sigma Green Belt (certified 2019)
jd: Mid-level Manufacturing Process Engineer for an injection-molding plant running three shifts across 14 presses and two automated assembly cells. You will own OEE for your value stream (currently 61%, plant target 75%), lead changeover-reduction (SMED) projects, and run structured problem-solving (8D, DMAIC) on the plant's top scrap and downtime drivers. The role is hands-on at the line: you are expected to personally lead kaizen events, write standard work, and drive measurable, sustained improvement — not just facilitate. Success in year one means a documented OEE gain on at least one cell with the financial impact quantified, plus a repeatable changeover standard adopted by operators. You will report to the Plant Engineering Manager and work daily with maintenance, quality, and shift supervisors.

history.q: Your resume lists a lot of methodologies. Pick the single project you're proudest of and walk me through it end to end.
On that SMED project — what was the changeover time before and after, and how did you get there?
Within that project, what specifically did YOU do that nobody else on the team did?

candidate_last_answer: Honestly it was a real team effort. We got everyone in a room, we mapped the changeover, and we just chipped away at it together. The operators had great ideas, maintenance helped us pre-stage the tooling, and quality signed off on the new standard. We brought the changeover down and we were all really proud of what we accomplished as a group. I don't like to single myself out — we won as a team.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: We brought the changeover down and we were all really proud of what we accomplished as a group

expected-yield: force the candidate to isolate his own individual contribution from the team's — the specific decision he made or analysis he personally ran on the SMED event, and the before/after changeover minutes attributable to that action rather than to operators or maintenance
